summary.headline_gap: No repo has pprof/flamegraph/memray/py-spy profiling instrumentation. Only focalpoint + thegent use criterion. Only OmniRoute has k6 load tests. No codspeed or divan in scope.

benchmarks.notes: Per-crate benches exist; no GitHub Actions job invokes `cargo bench`. Tooling/target-pruner (target-budget.toml) governs disk, not perf.

per_repo.notes: Infra/IaC-focused; docs/governance/cost-budget.md covers $ budgets, not runtime perf.

Several runtime-performance-baselines.md / ADR-009 / ADR-013 / test_performance.py / policystack regression-budget-gate scripts (e142, e144, e146, e149, e152) are declarative but not wired into a perf-budget CI gate job.
Library crate; no perf scaffolding detected.
Spec/journey authoring repo; not a runtime target.
Static sites/data; mentions Benchora in repos.json catalog only.

files: thegent/benchmark/* (routing_benchmark.py, scaled_benchmark.py, mcp_benchmark.py, sitback_benchmark.py, test_dispatch_benchmark.py, conftest.py, baseline.json, baseline-wl131-parse-model-suffix.json)
benchmarks.notes: Most mature L3 footprint. Baseline.json + wl131 baseline provide regression anchors. No CI workflow grepped invokes bench harness.

per_repo.notes: test_wl086_budget_tracker / test_wl108_context_budget / test_wl170_error_budget define budgets; not enforced by a perf-gate job.
k6-soak.js enforces http_req_failed<1% and p(95)<500ms thresholds; no CI workflow invokes it. Baselines (quality/file-size/test-discovery/duplication/complexity) are static, not perf.

key_gaps: Zero profiling tooling (pprof/flamegraph/memray/py-spy) across all 8 repos.
No codspeed, divan, locust, vegeta, or artillery anywhere in scope.
No repo wires a perf-budget or regression-threshold gate into GitHub Actions.
focalpoint criterion benches are local-only; never executed in CI.
OmniRoute k6-soak.js has thresholds defined but is not invoked by any workflow.
phenotype-tooling has the most regression-budget scripts but they are policystack-specific security budgets, not latency/throughput perf budgets.
recommendations: Add a `perf-ci.yml` to focalpoint that runs `cargo bench --workspace` and uploads criterion HTML/JSON; diff vs stored baseline via critcmp.
Wire OmniRoute/tests/load/k6-soak.js into a scheduled workflow; fail on http_req_failed >= 1% or p(95) >= 500ms.
Promote thegent baseline.json + wl131 into a CI gate (pytest-benchmark --benchmark-compare) and extend to Rust via criterion's `benches` baseline dir.
Add pprof sampling to a representative focalpoint service binary and memray to a representative thegent Python entrypoint.
Adopt codspeed for criterion in focalpoint + thegent to get deterministic perf assertions without flaky machine-noise.
Add a divan bench harness to any newly authored Rust crate to converge on one framework.
